operational_knowledge_units.title: Location model defines station locations with four location types
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide states that locations represent stations in the map and that the location model includes four types: Sorter, Tipper, Hospital, and Induction.
observed_evidence: Locations represent stations in the map.
Locations have 4 types. Sorter, Tipper, Hospital, Induction.
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Sorter Sweeper Database location model reference. Locations represent stations in the map. The model has four location types: Sorter, Tipper, Hospital, and Induction.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Induction and Hospital location meanings in the location model
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide defines Induction as the specified tote induction location at the hospital and Hospital as the specified tote drop-off location at the hospital.
observed_evidence: Induction: is the specified tote Induction location at the hospital.
Hospital: is the specified tote drop off location at the hospital.

relationships.to: specified tote induction location at the hospital
specified tote drop off location at the hospital

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Location model definitions: Induction is the specified tote induction location at the hospital. Hospital is the specified tote drop-off location at the hospital.

operational_knowledge_units.title: AGV cell code matching hospital or induction location triggers hospital controller notification
operational_knowledge_units.summary: When an AGV reports a cell code that matches either the induction or hospital location, the system notifies the hospital controller of the AGV's presence.
observed_evidence: When an AGV reports the same cell code as induction or hospital we notify the hospital controller of the presence of an AGV.
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: System behavior for hospital presence detection: if an AGV reports the same cell code as the induction or hospital location, the hospital controller is notified of the AGV presence.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Completed robotics task station is used to identify tipper and notify tote arrival
operational_knowledge_units.summary: For tippers, when a task is complete from the robotics engine, the reported station is used to determine which tipper the tote is at, and that specific tipper is notified that a tote has arrived.
observed_evidence: Tippers: when a task is complete from robotics engine we use the station it reported back to determine which tipper its at and notify that specific tipper a tote has arrived.

relationships.to: which tipper the tote is at

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Tipper routing rule: when a robotics engine task completes, use the station reported back to determine the tipper location and notify that specific tipper that a tote has arrived.

procedure_candidate_attempts.title: No explicit reusable procedure on location model slide
preconditions: Slide contains descriptive database model and behavior statements.
procedure_candidate_attempts.procedure_visual_summary: Text slide describing location types and event-triggered notifications for hospital and tippers; no numbered or imperative steps are shown.
warnings: Do not infer operator or support actions from system behavior statements.
The slide describes model semantics and automatic notifications, not a user-executed workflow.
procedure_candidate_attempts.retrieval_text: Discarded as a procedure candidate because slide 92 provides database model definitions and automatic system behavior for AGV and tipper notifications, but no explicit action sequence or reusable operational steps.
